note: Generated by `cargo xtask cli-help`. Read this file to understand all CLI commands before updating README.md or writing documentation.

commands.help:
Git-native secrets manager

Usage: gitvault [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  encrypt       Encrypt a secret file
  decrypt       Decrypt a .age encrypted file
  materialize   Materialize secrets to root .env
  status        Check repository safety status
  harden        Harden repository (update .gitignore, install hooks)
  run           Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables (REQ-21..25)
  allow-prod    Write a timed production allow token (REQ-14)
  merge-driver  Run as git merge driver for .env files (REQ-34) Usage: git config merge.gitvault-env.driver "gitvault merge-driver %O %A %B"
  recipient     Manage persistent recipients (REQ-37)
  rotate        Re-encrypt all secrets with the current recipients list (REQ-38)
  keyring       Manage identity key in OS keyring (REQ-39)
  check         Run preflight validation without side effects (REQ-50)
  revoke-prod   Revoke the production allow token immediately [REQ-14]
  identity      Manage local identity keys (REQ-61..63)
  help          Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help
  -V, --version
          Print version

Encrypt a secret file

Usage: gitvault encrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  File to encrypt

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
  -r, --recipient <RECIPIENTS>
          Recipient age public key (repeat for multi-recipient; defaults to local identity if omitted)
      --keep-path
          Preserve input path relative to repo root under secrets/<env>/
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --fields <FIELDS>
          Fields to encrypt (comma-separated key paths, for JSON/YAML/TOML field-level encryption; REQ-4)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --value-only
          Encrypt .env values individually instead of whole-file (REQ-6)
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Decrypt a .age encrypted file

Usage: gitvault decrypt [OPTIONS] <FILE>

Arguments:
  <FILE>  Encrypted .age file to decrypt

Options:
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path (or use `GITVAULT_IDENTITY` env var)
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
  -o, --output [<OUTPUT>]
          Output file path (default: strip .age extension)
      --fields <FIELDS>
          Fields to decrypt (comma-separated key paths, for JSON/YAML/TOML; REQ-4)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --reveal
          Print decrypted content to stdout instead of writing to file (REQ-41)
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
      --value-only
          Decrypt .env values individually (reverse of --value-only encrypt; REQ-6)
  -h, --help
          Print help

Materialize secrets to root .env

Usage: gitvault materialize [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use (overrides `SECRETS_ENV` and .secrets/env)
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --prod
          Require production barrier for prod env (REQ-13)
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Check repository safety status

Usage: gitvault status [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --fail-if-dirty
          Fail with exit code 6 if secrets/ has uncommitted changes (REQ-32)
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>

      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Run a command with secrets injected as environment variables (REQ-21..25)

Usage: gitvault run [OPTIONS] -- <COMMAND>...

Arguments:
  <COMMAND>...  Command and arguments to run

Options:
  -e, --env <ENV>
          Environment to use
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
  -i, --identity <IDENTITY>
          Identity key file path (or use `GITVAULT_IDENTITY` env var)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --prod
          Require production barrier (required when env=prod) (REQ-13)
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --clear-env
          Start child with empty environment (REQ-24)
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
      --pass <VARS>
          Comma-separated vars to pass through when --clear-env is set (REQ-24)
  -h, --help
          Print help

Write a timed production allow token (REQ-14)

Usage: gitvault allow-prod [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --ttl <TTL>
          Token lifetime in seconds [default: 3600]
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

Run as git merge driver for .env files (REQ-34) Usage: git config merge.gitvault-env.driver "gitvault merge-driver %O %A %B"

Usage: gitvault merge-driver [OPTIONS] <BASE> <OURS> <THEIRS>

Arguments:
  <BASE>    Base version (ancestor)
  <OURS>    Ours version (current branch, will be overwritten with merge result)
  <THEIRS>  Theirs version (incoming branch)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help

  remove  Remove a recipient public key
  list    List current recipients

      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>

          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]

Manage identity key in OS keyring (REQ-39)

Usage: gitvault keyring [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

  set     Store identity key in OS keyring
  get     Show public key of stored identity
  delete  Remove stored identity from OS keyring

          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)

Manage local identity keys (REQ-61..63)

Usage: gitvault identity [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  create  Create a new identity key (REQ-61, REQ-62)
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)

          Print help

Create a new identity key (REQ-61, REQ-62)

Usage: gitvault identity create [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json
          Output results as JSON (REQ-45)
      --profile <PROFILE>
          Identity profile: classic (age X25519) or hybrid (age X25519 + future PQ-ready label) [default: classic] [possible values: classic, hybrid]
      --no-prompt
          Disable interactive prompts (REQ-46)
      --out <PATH>
          Export identity to file (optional; default: store in OS keyring)
      --identity-selector <IDENTITY_SELECTOR>
          Explicit identity selector for SSH-agent key disambiguation (REQ-39/46) [env: GITVAULT_IDENTITY_SELECTOR=]
      --aws-profile <AWS_PROFILE>
          AWS profile name for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_PROFILE=]
      --aws-role-arn <AWS_ROLE_ARN>
          AWS role ARN to assume for SSM backend (REQ-49) [env: AWS_ROLE_ARN=]
  -h, --help
          Print help
